data.title: Inventor creates disposable breast pump to offer convenience, reduce cleaning. Solves issue with modern pumps.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on July 31, 2025, an inventor from Folsom, California, shared their idea for a more convenient breast pump option. Tired of the inconvenience and maintenance requirements of traditional breast pumps, they devised a disposable alternative. The new DISPOSABLE BREAST PUMP aims to simplify the pumping process for women. The invention addresses the need for increased convenience and eliminates the need for regular cleaning associated with current breast pump models.

data.title: 25 people hospitalized after Delta flight with 275 passengers from Salt Lake City to Amsterdam encountered severe turbulence, diverted to Minneapolis.
data.description: According to the airline, a flight carrying 275 passengers from Salt Lake City to Amsterdam had to make an emergency landing in Minneapolis on Wednesday evening.

data.title: Launch of Crew-11 mission commanded by NASA astronaut Zena Cardman delayed due to bad weather, postponing her first trip to space.
data.description: Zena Cardman, the NASA astronaut leading the Crew-11 mission, will have to endure a further delay before experiencing her maiden journey into orbit.

data.title: Tariffs plus factory shift lead to slow down in China. Light-industry factories face crippling tariffs post U.S.-China cease-fire.
data.description: The truce between the United States and China resulted in significant tariffs for exporters, further exacerbating a downturn for light industry factories due to China's transition to more advanced manufacturing practices.

data.title: Trump unveils new health records system for consumers, partnering with tech companies to facilitate sharing data. Privacy concerns arise.
data.description: The government is collaborating with technology firms to facilitate the sharing of data with multiple service providers. Privacy and security issues have been highlighted by experts during the discussions.

data.title: Treasury Secretary suggests Trump accounts may lead to privatizing Social Security, a move touching a divisive issue.
data.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent made controversial remarks regarding the safety net program, touching on a highly contentious issue in American politics.

data.title: Kim Kardashian unveils new face shapewear in latest Skims launch.
data.description: In a recent development, it has been reported that a total of 500 people have been affected by the new policy changes in the healthcare sector, resulting in 300 individuals losing their insurance coverage. The adjustments, which were implemented last month, are said to have generated mixed reactions among the public. The authorities have stated that these modifications were necessary to improve the efficiency of the system and ensure better access to medical services for all citizens. However, critics argue that the sudden decrease in insurance coverage has created difficulties for many families. The government has promised to address these concerns and work towards finding a suitable solution for those impacted by the changes. Further updates on this situation are expected in the coming weeks.

data.title: Global Oncology Diagnostics market set to hit $101.5B by 2029 due to rising investments and increasing cancer burden.
data.description: By 2030, cancer is set to surpass all other causes as the leading factor behind premature deaths worldwide. The projected global economic burden related to cancer is expected to reach a staggering $25.2 trillion by the year 2050. These alarming numbers have sparked increased investments, strategic innovations, and a surge in demand for oncology diagnostics globally. This market expansion trend is being fueled by the urgent need to address the growing impact of cancer on populations. These developments were highlighted in a report from Boston on July 31, 2025.

data.title: SEC introduces 'Project Crypto' to update regulations for blockchain-based financial systems. Led by Commissioner Hester Peirce.
data.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has introduced a new initiative named "Project Crypto" aimed at revamping securities regulations to align with blockchain-based financial systems. SEC Chair Paul Atkins revealed the project in a speech on Thursday, following a report released by the President's Working Group on Wednesday. This group includes top officials like Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick, in addition to Atkins. Project Crypto will be overseen by Commissioner Hester Peirce and a specialized task force she leads. For detailed information, please refer to Benzinga.com.

data.title: Align Technology Inc. reported a 3.4% increase in second-quarter sales to $1.01 billion, missing estimates. Clear Aligner revenue fell by 3.3% to $804.6 million while Imaging Systems revenue rose by 5.6% to $207.8 million. CEO mentioned uneven patient case conversion affecting results due to lower case starts late in the quarter. Tariff impacts and financing issues were also noted.
data.description: Align Technology Inc. reported disappointing second-quarter results, with sales increasing 3.4% sequentially but down 1.6% year-over-year to $1.01 billion, missing estimates of $1.06 billion. Adjusted earnings were $2.49 per share, below estimates of $2.57. Clear Aligner revenue was down 3.3% to $804.6 million, while Imaging Systems and CAD/CAM Services revenue increased 5.6% to $207.8 million. President and CEO Joe Hogan attributed the mixed results to uneven patient case conversion and lower-than-typical seasonal case starts. The company cited U.S. tariff turmoil and financing challenges as factors impacting quarterly results.

data.title: göt2b launches immersive pop-ups in NYC to showcase göt2b® Curled collection, celebrating waves and curls.
data.description: Henkel's innovative styling brand, göt2b®, is launching the göt2b® Curled collection through engaging activations to celebrate all types of curls. The brand, based in Stamford, Conn., is introducing the collection on July 31, 2025. Henkel's göt2b® brand offers a range of hair color and styling products to cater to diverse hair textures and styles.

data.title: Eric Walters appointed as General Manager of KNOE & KAQY in Monroe, LA by Gray Media, starting September 1, 2025.
data.description: In Atlanta on July 31, 2025, Gray Media announced Eric Walters as the upcoming General Manager of KNOE and KAQY-LD in Monroe, Louisiana. This appointment will be effective from September 1, 2025. KNOE and KAQY-LD are Gray's CBS and ABC affiliates in Monroe.

data.title: Tenable Holdings beats Q2 earnings estimates, reports EPS of 34 cents & sales of $247.29 million. FY25 guidance raised.
data.description: Tenable Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: TENB) exceeded expectations in their second-quarter financial report, announcing earnings of 34 cents per share, surpassing the estimated 30 cents per share. The company also revealed a quarterly sales figure of $247.29 million, higher than the anticipated $242.12 million. Tenable raised their FY2025 adjusted EPS guidance from $1.44-$1.52 to $1.45-$1.53 and increased sales guidance from $970.00 million-$980.00 million to $981.00 million-$987.00 million. Co-CEO, Steve Vintz, credited this success to a 12% revenue growth and a 19% operating margin, propelled by the increasing adoption of their exposure management platform by customers.

data.title: Inventor from Darby, Pa. creates new head covering called Royal Priesthood combining hat & durag benefits.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on July 31, 2025, a resident of Darby, Pennsylvania shared his motivation for creating a unique product. The inventor aimed to combine the advantages of a hat and a durag into one item, resulting in the development of the Royal Priesthood. This innovative design not only enhances style but also simplifies the user experience by consolidating the function of both headwear pieces.

data.title: The Rudolph Libbe Group ranks #1 on Crain's NE Ohio contractor list with highest 2024 revenue.
data.description: In a historic milestone, the multi-trade construction firm based in North Ridgeville, Ohio has secured the top spot in Northeast Ohio as the leading contractor ranked by 2024 revenue. The Rudolph Libbe Group of Companies achieved this prestigious No. 1 ranking for the first time, as announced by Crain's Cleveland Business on July 31, 2025. This significant accomplishment highlights the firm's growth and success, underscoring its position as a key player in the construction industry.
